WARNING: These test points are for A2779 (820-02655) ONLY. Component designators differ from A2442. This board uses AUSTRINGER (not Viper) and CLVR (not Monaco). Do NOT apply A2442 diagnostics to this board.
data_source: Schematic analysis from 163-page J414-C PDF. No Saf repair data yet for this board.

faults.name: PPBUS_AON short
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all M-series boards
faults.fix: Diode mode confirms short. Remove suspect cap. Retest.
faults.test_point: PPBUS_AON decoupling caps near charger IC area

faults.name: CD3217 liquid damage
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all USB-C MacBooks
faults.root_cause: Liquid ingress under CD3217 or level shifter ICs.
faults.fix: Clean board. Replace affected CD3217. Check level shifters on pages 80 (ATC0/1) and 88 (ATC2).

faults.name: Austringer VR (U9300) failure -- replaces Viper on A2442
faults.root_cause: Austringer VR controller dead or power stage failure. Board partially boots but PCPU SRAM domains don't power up.
faults.fix: If PVRVD0N_FAULT_L is low -> Austringer has detected a fault. Check 5 power stage pages (49-51). If output has no voltage -> U9300 dead.
faults.note: This is the A2779 equivalent of the A2442 Viper fault. Same diagnostic approach, different IC name.

faults.name: CLVR instance failure -- replaces Monaco on A2442
faults.affected_rail: Various CLVR outputs (PCPU, GPU, DPL, ANE domains)
faults.root_cause: CLVR VR instance not starting. SoC dynamic domain not powered.
faults.fix: Check each CLVR instance. Max variant: CLVR2 failure = GPU1 dead. Test each output. If all CLVR dead -> check CLVR supply (PP1V8_S1_CLVR_VDDH, PP0V8_S2_CLVR_VDDDIG from MPMU/SPMU LDOs).
faults.note: 5 CLVR instances vs 3 Monaco on A2442. More failure points but isolated -- one CLVR can fail without others.

faults.name: U5700 SVR AON failure (42A design)
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all M-series boards
faults.component: U5700 (SVR AON, 42A -- higher current than A2442's 30A)
faults.root_cause: SVR AON controller dead, Iceman gate driver phase fault, or downstream short on PP3V8_AON.
faults.fix: If output = 0V -> check PPVAON_PWR_EN. If enable asserted but no output -> U5700 dead. If output shorted -> thermal cam PP3V8_AON branches. Note: 3 Iceman gate driver phases (page 140 desense caps).

faults.name: MPMU (U8100) failure
faults.affected_rail: All MPMU buck and LDO outputs (BUCK0-10, LDO0-4)
faults.root_cause: Dead MPMU, liquid damage on BGA, or downstream short loading outputs.
faults.test_points: MPMU output caps (page 41), PMU test points (page 137)
faults.fix: If multiple outputs dead -> replace U8100. If single output dead -> find downstream short on that rail. Note: MPMU BUCK 2 is UNUSED on this board.

faults.name: Dual SSD / Ocarina fault
faults.root_cause: SSD0 or SSD1 NAND failure, Ocarina controller dead.
faults.fix: Check both SSD power rails independently. NAND CLK test points on page 113. Try DFU restore.
faults.note: Dual SSD with 4 NAND S5E packages each.

faults.name: HDMI Cobra controller fault
faults.fix: Board boots normally. HDMI dead. Check Cobra power rails (pages 95-96), HDMI DDC I2C, HPD.

faults.name: Display power sequencer fault (UP710)
faults.component: UP710 (SLGAP5157V display power sequencer, page 117), UP800 (Luxe backlight, page 118)
faults.fix: Check LCD_PWR_EN from SoC. Check UP710 output to UP701 (3V8 MDB feed) and UP730 (3V29 backlight feed). If sequencer outputs dead -> replace UP710.

faults.name: Willamette WiFi/BT module fault
faults.fix: Check PPVBAT_AON_WLBT and PP3V8_AON_WLBT supply rails. Check antenna connectors (page 102). Willamette is WiFi 6E.
